title: Permanent specimen preparation by protargol staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"47h7p","text":"Protargol staining according to the protocol by Bodian (Bodian 1936) and later modified by Nie (Nie 1950), further optimized for staining of small flagellates.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Mix 1 μm of culture sediment with a drop of sterile egg albumin, on a coverslip. For Solarion arienae , egg albumin was not diluted to achieve better adherence to the cells to coverslip.
Fix in Bouin-Hollande’s solution overnight. (0,175 mol/l picric acid, 0,138 mol/l Cu +2 acetate, 4% formaldehyde, and 5% acetic acid in water solution)
Next day, transfer coverslips throught a graded ethanol series (70%, 50%).
Wash in distilled water.
Bleach in 0,5% KMnO 4 solution for 5 minutes.
Wash in distilled water (5x).
Treat coverslips with 5% oxalic acid for 5 minutes.
Wash again in distilled water (5x).

Stain in 1% protargol solution, for 48 hours at 37°C in a beaker with copper wire pieces placed between the coverslips. For Solarion , protargol produced by Bayer, I. G. Farbenindustrie Actinengesellschaft (out of business since 1952) was used.
Wash in distilled water (2x).
Treat with freshly prepared reducing solution (1% hydroquinone and 5% Na 2 SO 3 ), for 10 minutes.
Wash in distilled water (5x).
Tone with 1% AuCl 3, for 5 minutes.
Wash in distilled water (2x).
Treat with 2% oxalic acid, for 5 minutes.
Wash in distilled water (5x).
Treat with 5% Na 2 S 2 O 3 for 10 minutes
The final wash is done under a constant stream of tap water for 20 minutes.
Dehydrate the coverslips in an ethanol series (50%, 70%, 80%, 96%, 100%)and 3 times in xylene (5 minutes each).
Finally, the stained, dehydrated coverslips are mounted on glass slides with DPX mountingmedium (Sigma–Aldrich).
Dehydrate in xylene, 3 times (5 minutes each).
creator: Marek Valt
